exam_traps.trap: Snowflake CLI (snow) and Schemachange are different tools for different purposes
exam_traps.why_confusing: Snowflake CLI is the official tool for deploying Snowpark, Streamlit, and managing Snowflake objects. Schemachange is a third-party tool for database migration scripts (similar to Flyway). The exam may test which tool for which use case.
exam_traps.correct_answer: Snowflake CLI = deploy apps/objects. Schemachange = database migration scripts.

questions.question: An Architect needs to deploy schema changes to production with zero downtime. Which Snowflake feature enables this?
questions.overall_explanation: ALTER DATABASE SWAP WITH atomically swaps two databases, enabling blue/green deployments. Deploy changes to the 'blue' database, test them, then swap it with production. If issues arise, swap back for instant rollback.

questions.question: What authentication method is recommended for Snowflake CI/CD pipelines?

options.text: Username and password stored in environment variables.
Key-pair authentication with an encrypted private key.

questions.overall_explanation: Key-pair authentication is the recommended method for CI/CD pipelines. It avoids storing passwords, supports key rotation with two active keys, and doesn't require interactive login. SSO and OAuth with consent flows are interactive and unsuitable for automated pipelines.

questions.question: Which approach is recommended for rolling back a failed production deployment in Snowflake? (Choose two.)

options.text: Use ALTER DATABASE SWAP WITH to swap back to the previous version.
Manually re-run the previous migration scripts.
Use Time Travel to clone the database from before the deployment.
Delete the account and recreate it.

questions.overall_explanation: Two reliable rollback strategies: (1) ALTER DATABASE SWAP WITH reverses a blue/green deployment instantly. (2) Time Travel allows cloning a database from a point before the failed deployment. Both are fast and don't require re-running scripts.
